  <p></p>

<p>Scroll-driven animations are a common UX pattern on the web. A scroll-driven animation is linked to the scroll position of a scroll container. This means that as you scroll up or down, the linked animation scrubs forward or backward in direct response. Examples of this are effects such as parallax background images or reading indicators which move as you scroll.</p>

<figure>
  <video controls="" height="800" playsinline="" width="800" style="--vid-width: 800; --vid-height: 800"> <source src="/static/docs/css-ui/scroll-driven-animations/video/AeNB0cHNDkYPUYzDuv8gInYA9rY2/HiTavHlNjHFrA3NLLDZq.mp4" type="video/mp4"> </video>
  <figcaption class="dcc-caption">A reading indicator atop a document, driven by scroll.</figcaption>
</figure>

<p>A similar type of scroll-driven animation is an animation that is linked to an element's position within its scroll container. With it, for example, elements can fade-in as they come into view.</p>

<figure>
  <video controls="" height="800" playsinline="" width="800" style="--vid-width: 800; --vid-height: 800"> <source src="/static/docs/css-ui/scroll-driven-animations/video/AeNB0cHNDkYPUYzDuv8gInYA9rY2/eV0R7BXHO7ieTFVBfTEE.mp4" type="video/mp4"> </video>
  <figcaption class="dcc-caption">The images on this page fade-in as they come into view.</figcaption>
</figure>

<p>The classic way to achieve these kinds of effects is to respond to scroll events on <a href="https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Glossary/Main_thread">the main thread</a>, which leads to two main problems:</p>

<ul>
<li>Modern browsers perform scrolling on a separate process and therefore deliver scroll events asynchronously.</li>
<li>Main thread animations are <a href="/blog/inside-browser-part3#updating_rendering_pipeline_is_costly">subject to jank</a>.</li>
</ul>

<p>This makes creating performant scroll-driven animations that are in-sync with scrolling impossible or very difficult.</p>

<p>From Chrome version 115 there is a new set of APIs and concepts that you can use to enable declarative scroll-driven animations: Scroll Timelines and View Timelines.</p>

<p>These new concepts integrate with the existing <a href="https://drafts.csswg.org/web-animations-1/">Web Animations API (WAAPI)</a> and <a href="https://drafts.csswg.org/css-animations-1/">CSS Animations API</a>, allowing them to inherit the advantages these existing APIs bring. That includes the ability to have scroll-driven animations run off the main thread. Yes, read that correctly: you can now have silky smooth animations, driven by scroll, running off the main thread, with just a few lines of extra code. What's not to like?!</p>

<aside class="note"><b>Note:</b> If you can’t wait to check out some demos go visit <a href="https://scroll-driven-animations.style/">https://scroll-driven-animations.style</a>, a site packed with demos and tools to check out.</aside>

<h2 id="animations_on_the_web_a_small_recap" data-text="Animations on the web, a small recap" tabindex="-1">Animations on the web, a small recap</h2>

<h3 id="animations_on_the_web_with_css" data-text="Animations on the web with CSS" tabindex="-1">Animations on the web with CSS</h3>

<p>To create an animation in CSS, define a set of keyframes using the <code translate="no" dir="ltr">@keyframes</code> at-rule. Link it up to an element using the <code translate="no" dir="ltr">animation-name</code> property while also setting an <code translate="no" dir="ltr">animation-duration</code> to determine how long the animation should take. There are more <code translate="no" dir="ltr">animation-*</code> longhand properties available–<code translate="no" dir="ltr">animation-easing-function</code> and <code translate="no" dir="ltr">animation-fill-mode</code> just to name a few–which can all be combined in the <code translate="no" dir="ltr">animation</code> shorthand.</p>

<p>For example, here’s an animation that scales up an element on the X-axis while also changing its background color:</p>
<div></div><devsite-code data-copy-event-label=""><pre class="" translate="no" dir="ltr" is-upgraded="" syntax="CSS"><code translate="no" dir="ltr"><span class="devsite-syntax-p">@</span><span class="devsite-syntax-k">keyframes</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-nt">scale-up</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">{</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-w">  </span><span class="devsite-syntax-nt">from</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">{</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-w">    </span><span class="devsite-syntax-k">background-color</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">:</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-kc">red</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">;</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-w">    </span><span class="devsite-syntax-k">transform</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">:</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-nb">scaleX</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">(</span><span class="devsite-syntax-mi">0</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">);</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-w">  </span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">}</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-w">  </span><span class="devsite-syntax-nt">to</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">{</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-w">    </span><span class="devsite-syntax-k">background-color</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">:</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-kc">darkred</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">;</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-w">    </span><span class="devsite-syntax-k">transform</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">:</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-nb">scaleX</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">(</span><span class="devsite-syntax-mi">1</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">);</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-w">  </span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">}</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-p">}</span>

<span class="devsite-syntax-p">#</span><span class="devsite-syntax-nn">progressbar</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">{</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-w">  </span><span class="devsite-syntax-k">animation</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">:</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-mf">2.5</span><span class="devsite-syntax-kt">s</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-kc">linear</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-kc">forwards</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-k">scale</span><span class="devsite-syntax-o">-</span><span class="devsite-syntax-n">up</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">;</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-p">}</span>
</code></pre></devsite-code>
<aside class="note"><b>Note:</b> To learn more about CSS Animations, visit <a href="https://web.dev/learn/css/animations">Learn CSS Animations</a></aside>

<h3 id="animations_on_the_web_with_javascript" data-text="Animations on the web with JavaScript" tabindex="-1">Animations on the web with JavaScript</h3>

<p>In JavaScript, the Web Animations API can be used to achieve exactly the same. You can do this by either creating new <code translate="no" dir="ltr">Animation</code> and <code translate="no" dir="ltr">KeyFrameEffect</code> instances, or use the much shorter <a href="https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/API/Element/animate"><code translate="no" dir="ltr">Element</code> <code translate="no" dir="ltr">animate()</code> method</a>.</p>
<div></div><devsite-code data-copy-event-label=""><pre class="" translate="no" dir="ltr" is-upgraded="" syntax="JavaScript"><code translate="no" dir="ltr"><span class="devsite-syntax-nb">document</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">.</span><span class="devsite-syntax-nx">querySelector</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">(</span><span class="devsite-syntax-s1">'#progressbar'</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">).</span><span class="devsite-syntax-nx">animate</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">(</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-w">  </span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">{</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-w">    </span><span class="devsite-syntax-nx">backgroundColor</span><span class="devsite-syntax-o">:</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">[</span><span class="devsite-syntax-s1">'red'</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">,</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-s1">'darkred'</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">],</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-w">    </span><span class="devsite-syntax-nx">transform</span><span class="devsite-syntax-o">:</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">[</span><span class="devsite-syntax-s1">'scaleX(0)'</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">,</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-s1">'scaleX(1)'</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">],</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-w">  </span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">},</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-w">  </span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">{</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-w">    </span><span class="devsite-syntax-nx">duration</span><span class="devsite-syntax-o">:</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-mf">2500</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">,</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-w">    </span><span class="devsite-syntax-nx">fill</span><span class="devsite-syntax-o">:</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-s1">'forwards'</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">,</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-w">    </span><span class="devsite-syntax-nx">easing</span><span class="devsite-syntax-o">:</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-s1">'linear'</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">,</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-w">   </span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">}</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-p">);</span>
</code></pre></devsite-code>
<p>This visual result of the JavaScript snippet above is identical to the previous CSS version.</p>

<h2 id="animation_timelines" data-text="Animation timelines" tabindex="-1">Animation timelines</h2>

<p>By default, an animation attached to an element runs on the <a href="https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/API/DocumentTimeline">document timeline</a>. Its origin time starts at 0 when the page loads, and starts ticking forwards as clock time progresses. This is the default animation timeline and, until now, was the only animation timeline you had access to.</p>

<p>The <a href="https://drafts.csswg.org/scroll-animations-1/">Scroll-driven Animations Specification</a> defines two new types of timelines that you can use:</p>

<ul>
<li><strong>Scroll Progress Timeline</strong>: a timeline that is linked to the scroll position of a scroll container along a particular axis.</li>
<li><strong>View Progress Timeline</strong>: a timeline that is linked to the relative position of a particular element within its scroll container.</li>
</ul>

<h3 id="scroll_progress_timeline" data-text="Scroll Progress Timeline" tabindex="-1">Scroll Progress Timeline</h3>

<p>A Scroll Progress Timeline is an animation timeline that is linked to progress in the scroll position of a scroll container–also called <em>scrollport</em> or <em>scroller</em>–along a particular axis. It converts a position in a scroll range into a percentage of progress.</p>

<p>The starting scroll position represents 0% progress and the ending scroll position represents 100% progress. In the following visualization, you can see that the progress counts up from 0% to 100% as you scroll the scroller from top to bottom.</p>

<figure>
  <video controls="" height="800" playsinline="" width="800" style="--vid-width: 800; --vid-height: 800"> <source src="/static/docs/css-ui/scroll-driven-animations/video/AeNB0cHNDkYPUYzDuv8gInYA9rY2/xdU4YJ6cxjNYpec1XcE6.mp4" type="video/mp4"> </video>
  <figcaption class="dcc-caption">Visualization of a Scroll Progress Timeline. As you scroll down to the bottom of the scroller, the progress value counts up from 0% to 100%.</figcaption>
</figure>

<p>✨ Try it for yourself
<iframe loading="lazy" src="https://scroll-driven-animations.style/tools/scroll-timeline/progress/?embed" frameborder="0" sandbox="allow-scripts allow-forms allow-top-navigation" width="500" height="500" style="height: 500px; width: 100%; border: 1px solid #333;"></iframe></p>

<p>A Scroll Progress Timeline is often abbreviated to simply “Scroll Timeline”.</p>

<h3 id="view_progress_timeline" data-text="View Progress Timeline" tabindex="-1">View Progress Timeline</h3>

<p>This type of timeline is linked to the relative progress of a particular element within a scroll container. Just like a Scroll Progress Timeline, a scroller’s scroll offset is tracked. Unlike a Scroll Progress Timeline, it’s the relative position of a subject within that scroller that determines the progress.</p>

<p>This is somewhat comparable to how <a href="https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/API/Intersection_Observer_API"><code translate="no" dir="ltr">IntersectionObserver</code></a> works, which can track how much an element is visible in the scroller. If the element is not visible in the scroller, it is not intersecting. If it is visible inside the scroller–even for the smallest part–it is intersecting.</p>

<p>A View Progress Timeline begins from the moment a subject starts intersecting with the scroller and ends when the subject stops intersecting the scroller. In the following visualization, you can see that the progress starts counting up from 0% when the subject enters the scroll container and reaches 100% at the very moment the subject has left the scroll container.</p>

<figure>
  <video controls="" height="800" playsinline="" width="800" style="--vid-width: 800; --vid-height: 800"> <source src="/static/docs/css-ui/scroll-driven-animations/video/AeNB0cHNDkYPUYzDuv8gInYA9rY2/rvPTFW2277KBTuWiZFj1.mp4" type="video/mp4"> </video>
  <figcaption class="dcc-caption">Visualization of a View Progress Timeline. The progress counts up from 0% to 100% as the subject (green box) crosses the scroller.</figcaption>
</figure>

<p>✨ Try it for yourself
<iframe loading="lazy" src="https://scroll-driven-animations.style/tools/view-timeline/progress/?embed" frameborder="0" sandbox="allow-scripts allow-forms allow-top-navigation" width="500" height="500" style="height: 500px; width: 100%; border: 1px solid #333;"></iframe></p>

<p>A View Progress Timeline is often abbreviated to simply “View Timeline”. It is possible to target specific parts of a View Timeline based on the subject’s size, but more on that later.</p>

<h2 id="getting_practical_with_scroll_progress_timelines" data-text="Getting practical with Scroll Progress Timelines" tabindex="-1">Getting practical with Scroll Progress Timelines</h2>

<h3 id="creating_an_anonymous_scroll_progress_timeline_in_css" data-text="Creating an anonymous Scroll Progress Timeline in CSS" tabindex="-1">Creating an anonymous Scroll Progress Timeline in CSS</h3>

<p>The easiest way to create a Scroll Timeline in CSS is to use the <code translate="no" dir="ltr">scroll()</code> function. This creates an anonymous Scroll Timeline that you can set as the value for the new <code translate="no" dir="ltr">animation-timeline</code> property.</p>

<p>Example:</p>
<div></div><devsite-code data-copy-event-label=""><pre class="" translate="no" dir="ltr" is-upgraded="" syntax="CSS"><code translate="no" dir="ltr"><span class="devsite-syntax-p">@</span><span class="devsite-syntax-k">keyframes</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-nt">animate-it</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">{</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-err">…</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">}</span>

<span class="devsite-syntax-p">.</span><span class="devsite-syntax-nc">subject</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">{</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-w">  </span><span class="devsite-syntax-k">animation</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">:</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-n">animate-it</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-kc">linear</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">;</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-w">  </span><span class="devsite-syntax-n">animation-timeline</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">:</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-nf">scroll</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">(</span><span class="devsite-syntax-n">root</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-kc">block</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">);</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-p">}</span>
</code></pre></devsite-code>
<aside class="note"><b>Note:</b> The <code translate="no" dir="ltr">animation-timeline</code> longhand property is not part of the <code translate="no" dir="ltr">animation</code> shorthand and must be declared separately. Furthermore, <code translate="no" dir="ltr">animation-timeline</code> must be declared after the <code translate="no" dir="ltr">animation</code> shorthand as the shorthand will reset non-included longhands to their initial value.</aside>

<p>The <code translate="no" dir="ltr">scroll()</code> function accepts a <code translate="no" dir="ltr"><scroller></code> and an <code translate="no" dir="ltr"><axis></code> argument.</p>

<p>Accepted values for the <code translate="no" dir="ltr"><scroller></code> argument are the following:</p>

<ul>
<li><code translate="no" dir="ltr">nearest</code>: Uses the nearest ancestor scroll container <em>(default)</em>.</li>
<li><code translate="no" dir="ltr">root</code>: Uses the document viewport as the scroll container.</li>
<li><code translate="no" dir="ltr">self</code>: Uses the element itself as the scroll container.</li>
</ul>

<p>Accepted values for the <code translate="no" dir="ltr"><axis></code> argument are the following:</p>

<ul>
<li><code translate="no" dir="ltr">block</code>: Uses the measure of progress along the block axis of the scroll container <em>(default)</em>.</li>
<li><code translate="no" dir="ltr">inline</code>: Uses the measure of progress along the inline axis of the scroll container.</li>
<li><code translate="no" dir="ltr">y</code>: Uses the measure of progress along the y axis of the scroll container.</li>
<li><code translate="no" dir="ltr">x</code>: Uses the measure of progress along the x axis of the scroll container.</li>
</ul>

<p>For example, to bind an animation to the root scroller on the block axis, the values to pass into <code translate="no" dir="ltr">scroll()</code> are <code translate="no" dir="ltr">root</code> and <code translate="no" dir="ltr">block</code>. Put together, the value is <code translate="no" dir="ltr">scroll(root block)</code>.</p>

<aside class="key-point"><b>Key point:</b> Because an <code translate="no" dir="ltr">animation-duration</code> set in seconds does not make sense when using a Scroll Progress Timeline, you must set <code translate="no" dir="ltr">animation-duration</code> to <code translate="no" dir="ltr">auto</code>. Alternatively, as done in the code snippet above, you can omit the <code translate="no" dir="ltr">animation-duration</code> from the <code translate="no" dir="ltr">animation</code> shorthand as it will then use its default value which is <code translate="no" dir="ltr">auto</code>.</aside>

<h4 id="demo_reading_progress_indicator" data-text="Demo: Reading progress indicator" tabindex="-1">Demo: Reading progress indicator</h4>

<p>This demo has a reading progress indicator fixed to the top of the viewport. As you scroll down the page, the progress bar grows until it takes up the full viewport width upon reaching the end of the document. An anonymous Scroll Progress Timeline is used to drive the animation.</p>

<figure>
  <video controls="" height="800" playsinline="" width="800" style="--vid-width: 800; --vid-height: 800"> <source src="/static/docs/css-ui/scroll-driven-animations/video/AeNB0cHNDkYPUYzDuv8gInYA9rY2/HiTavHlNjHFrA3NLLDZq.mp4" type="video/mp4"> </video>
  <figcaption class="dcc-caption">Demo: <a href="https://scroll-driven-animations.style/demos/progress-bar/css/scroll-defaults.html">Reading progress indicator</a>.</figcaption>
</figure>

<p>✨ Try it for yourself
<iframe loading="lazy" src="https://scroll-driven-animations.style/demos/progress-bar/css/scroll-defaults.html?embed" frameborder="0" sandbox="allow-scripts allow-forms allow-top-navigation" width="500" height="600" style="height: 600px; width: 100%; border: 1px solid #333;"></iframe></p>

<p>The reading progress indicator is positioned at the top of the page using position fixed. To leverage composited animations, not the <code translate="no" dir="ltr">width</code> is being animated but the element is scaled down on the x-axis using a <code translate="no" dir="ltr">transform</code>.</p>
<div></div><devsite-code data-copy-event-label=""><pre class="" translate="no" dir="ltr" is-upgraded="" syntax="HTML"><code translate="no" dir="ltr"><body>
  <div id="progress"></div>
  …
</body>
</code></pre></devsite-code><div></div><devsite-code data-copy-event-label=""><pre class="" translate="no" dir="ltr" is-upgraded="" syntax="CSS"><code translate="no" dir="ltr"><span class="devsite-syntax-p">@</span><span class="devsite-syntax-k">keyframes</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-nt">grow-progress</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">{</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-w">  </span><span class="devsite-syntax-nt">from</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">{</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-k">transform</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">:</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-nb">scaleX</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">(</span><span class="devsite-syntax-mi">0</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">);</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">}</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-w">  </span><span class="devsite-syntax-nt">to</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">{</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-k">transform</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">:</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-nb">scaleX</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">(</span><span class="devsite-syntax-mi">1</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">);</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">}</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-p">}</span>

<span class="devsite-syntax-p">#</span><span class="devsite-syntax-nn">progress</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">{</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-w">  </span><span class="devsite-syntax-k">position</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">:</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-kc">fixed</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">;</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-w">  </span><span class="devsite-syntax-k">left</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">:</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-mi">0</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">;</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-k">top</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">:</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-mi">0</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">;</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-w">  </span><span class="devsite-syntax-k">width</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">:</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-mi">100</span><span class="devsite-syntax-kt">%</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">;</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-k">height</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">:</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-mi">1</span><span class="devsite-syntax-kt">em</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">;</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-w">  </span><span class="devsite-syntax-k">background</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">:</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-kc">red</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">;</span>

<span class="devsite-syntax-w">  </span><span class="devsite-syntax-k">transform-origin</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">:</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-mi">0</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-mi">50</span><span class="devsite-syntax-kt">%</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">;</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-w">  </span><span class="devsite-syntax-k">animation</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">:</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-n">grow-progress</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-kc">auto</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-kc">linear</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">;</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-w">  </span><span class="devsite-syntax-n">animation-timeline</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">:</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-nf">scroll</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">();</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-p">}</span>
</code></pre></devsite-code>
<p>The timeline for the animation <code translate="no" dir="ltr">grow-progress</code> on the <code translate="no" dir="ltr">#progress</code> element is set to an anonymous timeline that’s created using <code translate="no" dir="ltr">scroll()</code>. No arguments are given to <code translate="no" dir="ltr">scroll()</code> so it will fall back to its default values.</p>

<p>The default scroller to track is the <code translate="no" dir="ltr">nearest</code> one, and the default axis is <code translate="no" dir="ltr">block</code>. This effectively targets the root scroller as that is the nearest scroller of the <code translate="no" dir="ltr">#progress</code> element, while tracking its block direction.</p>

<h3 id="creating_a_named_scroll_progress_timeline_in_css" data-text="Creating a named Scroll Progress Timeline in CSS" tabindex="-1">Creating a named Scroll Progress Timeline in CSS</h3>

<p>An alternative way to define a Scroll Progress Timeline is to use a named one. It’s a bit more verbose, but it can come in handy when you aren’t targeting a parent scroller or the root scroller, or when the page uses multiple timelines or when automatic lookups don’t work. This way, you can identify a Scroll Progress Timeline by the name that you give it.</p>

<p>To create a named Scroll Progress Timeline on an element, set the <code translate="no" dir="ltr">scroll-timeline-name</code> CSS property on the scroll container to an identifier of your liking. The value must start with <code translate="no" dir="ltr">--</code>.</p>

<p>To tweak which axis to track, also declare the <code translate="no" dir="ltr">scroll-timeline-axis</code> property. Allowed values are the same as the <code translate="no" dir="ltr"><axis></code> argument of <code translate="no" dir="ltr">scroll()</code>.</p>

<p>Finally, to link the animation to the Scroll Progress Timeline, set the <code translate="no" dir="ltr">animation-timeline</code> property on the element that needs to be animated to the same value as the identifier used for the <code translate="no" dir="ltr">scroll-timeline-name</code>.</p>

<p>Code Example:</p>
<div></div><devsite-code data-copy-event-label=""><pre class="" translate="no" dir="ltr" is-upgraded="" syntax="CSS"><code translate="no" dir="ltr"><span class="devsite-syntax-p">@</span><span class="devsite-syntax-k">keyframes</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-nt">animate-it</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">{</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-err">…</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">}</span>

<span class="devsite-syntax-p">.</span><span class="devsite-syntax-nc">scroller</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">{</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-w">  </span><span class="devsite-syntax-n">scroll-timeline-name</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">:</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-o">--</span><span class="devsite-syntax-n">my-scroller</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">;</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-w">  </span><span class="devsite-syntax-n">scroll-timeline-axis</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">:</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-kc">inline</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">;</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-p">}</span>

<span class="devsite-syntax-p">.</span><span class="devsite-syntax-nc">scroller</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">.</span><span class="devsite-syntax-nc">subject</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">{</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-w">  </span><span class="devsite-syntax-k">animation</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">:</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-n">animate-it</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-kc">linear</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">;</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-w">  </span><span class="devsite-syntax-n">animation-timeline</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">:</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-o">--</span><span class="devsite-syntax-n">my-scroller</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">;</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-p">}</span>
</code></pre></devsite-code>
<p>If wanted, you can combine <code translate="no" dir="ltr">scroll-timeline-name</code> and <code translate="no" dir="ltr">scroll-timeline-axis</code> in the <code translate="no" dir="ltr">scroll-timeline</code> shorthand. For example:</p>
<div></div><devsite-code data-copy-event-label=""><pre class="" translate="no" dir="ltr" is-upgraded="" syntax="CSS"><code translate="no" dir="ltr"><span class="devsite-syntax-nt">scroll-timeline</span><span class="devsite-syntax-o">:</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-nt">--my-scroller</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-nt">inline</span><span class="devsite-syntax-o">;</span>
</code></pre></devsite-code>
<aside class="key-point"><b>Key point:</b> Note that even for named Scroll Timelines the lookup from the subject to the scroller happens across ancestors only. How to target a non-ancestor element, such as a sibling element, is covered further down this article in the <a href="#attaching_to_a_non-ancestor_scroll_timeline">“Attaching to a non-ancestor Scroll Timeline”</a> section.</aside>

<h4 id="demo_horizontal_carousel_step_indicator" data-text="Demo: Horizontal carousel step indicator" tabindex="-1">Demo: Horizontal carousel step indicator</h4>

<p>This demo features a step indicator shown above each image carousel. When a carousel contains three images, the indicator bar starts at 33% width to indicate you are currently looking at image one of three. When the last image is in view–determined by the scroller having scrolled to the end–the indicator takes up the full width of the scroller. A named Scroll Progress Timeline is used to drive the animation.</p>

<figure>
  <video controls="" height="800" playsinline="" width="800" style="--vid-width: 800; --vid-height: 800"> <source src="/static/docs/css-ui/scroll-driven-animations/video/AeNB0cHNDkYPUYzDuv8gInYA9rY2/5ln4aDkZ625xlM9jZXPl.mp4" type="video/mp4"> </video>
  <figcaption class="dcc-caption">Demo: <a href="https://scroll-driven-animations.style/demos/horizontal-carousel/css/">Horizontal carousel step indicator</a>.</figcaption>
</figure>

<p>✨ Try it for yourself
<iframe loading="lazy" src="https://scroll-driven-animations.style/demos/horizontal-carousel/css/?embed" frameborder="0" sandbox="allow-scripts allow-forms allow-top-navigation" width="500" height="600" style="height: 600px; width: 100%; border: 1px solid #333;"></iframe></p>

<p>The base markup for a gallery is this:</p>
<div></div><devsite-code data-copy-event-label=""><pre class="" translate="no" dir="ltr" is-upgraded="" syntax="HTML"><code translate="no" dir="ltr"><div class="gallery" style="--num-images: 2;">
  <div class="gallery__scrollcontainer">
    <div class="gallery__progress"></div>
    <div class="gallery__entry">…</div>
    <div class="gallery__entry">…</div>
  </div>
</div>
</code></pre></devsite-code>
<p>The <code translate="no" dir="ltr">.gallery__progress</code> element is absolutely positioned within the <code translate="no" dir="ltr">.gallery</code> wrapper element. Its initial size is determined by the <code translate="no" dir="ltr">--num-images</code> custom property.</p>
<div></div><devsite-code data-copy-event-label=""><pre class="" translate="no" dir="ltr" is-upgraded="" syntax="CSS"><code translate="no" dir="ltr"><span class="devsite-syntax-p">.</span><span class="devsite-syntax-nc">gallery</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">{</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-w">  </span><span class="devsite-syntax-k">position</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">:</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-kc">relative</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">;</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-p">}</span>


<span class="devsite-syntax-p">.</span><span class="devsite-syntax-nc">gallery__progress</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">{</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-w">  </span><span class="devsite-syntax-k">position</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">:</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-kc">absolute</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">;</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-w">  </span><span class="devsite-syntax-k">top</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">:</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-mi">0</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">;</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-w">  </span><span class="devsite-syntax-k">left</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">:</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-mi">0</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">;</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-w">  </span><span class="devsite-syntax-k">width</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">:</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-mi">100</span><span class="devsite-syntax-kt">%</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">;</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-w">  </span><span class="devsite-syntax-k">height</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">:</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-mi">1</span><span class="devsite-syntax-kt">em</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">;</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-w">  </span><span class="devsite-syntax-k">transform</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">:</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-nb">scaleX</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">(</span><span class="devsite-syntax-nb">calc</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">(</span><span class="devsite-syntax-mi">1</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-o">/</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-nf">var</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">(</span><span class="devsite-syntax-nv">--num-images</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">)));</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-p">}</span>
</code></pre></devsite-code>
<p>The <code translate="no" dir="ltr">.gallery__scrollcontainer</code> lays out the contained <code translate="no" dir="ltr">.gallery__entry</code> elements horizontally and is the element that scrolls. By tracking its scroll position, the <code translate="no" dir="ltr">.gallery__progress</code> gets animated. This is done by referring to the named Scroll Progress Timeline <code translate="no" dir="ltr">--gallery__scrollcontainer</code>.</p>
<div></div><devsite-code data-copy-event-label=""><pre class="" translate="no" dir="ltr" is-upgraded="" syntax="CSS"><code translate="no" dir="ltr"><span class="devsite-syntax-p">@</span><span class="devsite-syntax-k">keyframes</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-nt">grow-progress</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">{</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-w">  </span><span class="devsite-syntax-nt">to</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">{</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-k">transform</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">:</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-nb">scaleX</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">(</span><span class="devsite-syntax-mi">1</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">);</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">}</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-p">}</span>

<span class="devsite-syntax-p">.</span><span class="devsite-syntax-nc">gallery__scrollcontainer</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">{</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-w">  </span><span class="devsite-syntax-k">overflow-x</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">:</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-kc">scroll</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">;</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-w">  </span><span class="devsite-syntax-n">scroll-timeline</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">:</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-o">--</span><span class="devsite-syntax-n">gallery__scrollcontainer</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-kc">inline</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">;</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-p">}</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-p">.</span><span class="devsite-syntax-nc">gallery__progress</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">{</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-w">  </span><span class="devsite-syntax-k">animation</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">:</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-kc">auto</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-n">grow-progress</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-kc">linear</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-kc">forwards</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">;</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-w">  </span><span class="devsite-syntax-n">animation-timeline</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">:</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-o">--</span><span class="devsite-syntax-n">gallery__scrollcontainer</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">;</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-p">}</span>
</code></pre></devsite-code>
<aside class="warning"><b>Warning:</b> For this specific demo, using an Anonymous Scroll Progress Timeline would not have worked. If you set <code translate="no" dir="ltr">animation-timeline: scroll(nearest inline)</code> on <code translate="no" dir="ltr">.gallery__progress</code> it would not find the scroller from the <code translate="no" dir="ltr">.gallery__scrollcontainer</code> even if that element is its direct parent.

The reason for this, is that lookups for <code translate="no" dir="ltr">nearest</code> only considers the elements that can affect its position and size. Because <code translate="no" dir="ltr">.gallery__progress</code> is absolutely positioned, the first parent element that will determine its size and position is the <code translate="no" dir="ltr">.gallery</code> element as it has <code translate="no" dir="ltr">position: relative</code> applied, thereby jumping over the <code translate="no" dir="ltr">.gallery__scrollcontainer</code> element.

Expressed in more technical terms, the lookup walks up <a href="https://drafts.csswg.org/css-display/#containing-block-chain">the containing block chain</a> to find the nearest <a href="https://drafts.csswg.org/css-overflow-3/#scroll-container">scroll container</a>.</aside>

<h3 id="creating_a_scroll_progress_timeline_with_javascript" data-text="Creating a Scroll Progress Timeline with JavaScript" tabindex="-1">Creating a Scroll Progress Timeline with JavaScript</h3>

<p>To create a Scroll Timeline in JavaScript, create a new instance of the <code translate="no" dir="ltr">ScrollTimeline</code> class. Pass in a property bag with the <code translate="no" dir="ltr">source</code> and <code translate="no" dir="ltr">axis</code> that you want to track.</p>

<ul>
<li><code translate="no" dir="ltr">source</code>: A reference to the element whose scroller that you want to track. Use <code translate="no" dir="ltr">document.documentElement</code> to target the root scroller.</li>
<li><code translate="no" dir="ltr">axis</code>: Determines which axis to track. Similar to the CSS variant, accepted values are <code translate="no" dir="ltr">block</code>, <code translate="no" dir="ltr">inline</code>, <code translate="no" dir="ltr">x</code>, and <code translate="no" dir="ltr">y</code>.</li>
</ul>
<div></div><devsite-code data-copy-event-label=""><pre class="" translate="no" dir="ltr" is-upgraded="" syntax="JavaScript"><code translate="no" dir="ltr"><span class="devsite-syntax-kd">const</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-nx">tl</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-o">=</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-ow">new</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-nx">ScrollTimeline</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">({</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-w">  </span><span class="devsite-syntax-nx">source</span><span class="devsite-syntax-o">:</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-nb">document</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">.</span><span class="devsite-syntax-nx">documentElement</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">,</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-p">});</span>
</code></pre></devsite-code>
<p>To attach it to a Web Animation, pass it in as the <code translate="no" dir="ltr">timeline</code> property and omit any <code translate="no" dir="ltr">duration</code> if there was any.</p>
<div></div><devsite-code data-copy-event-label=""><pre class="" translate="no" dir="ltr" is-upgraded="" syntax="JavaScript"><code translate="no" dir="ltr"><span class="devsite-syntax-nx">$el</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">.</span><span class="devsite-syntax-nx">animate</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">({</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-w">  </span><span class="devsite-syntax-nx">opacity</span><span class="devsite-syntax-o">:</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">[</span><span class="devsite-syntax-mf">0</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">,</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-mf">1</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">],</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-p">},</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">{</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-w">  </span><span class="devsite-syntax-nx">timeline</span><span class="devsite-syntax-o">:</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-nx">tl</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">,</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-p">});</span>
</code></pre></devsite-code>
<h4 id="demo_reading_progress_indicator_revisited" data-text="Demo: Reading progress indicator, revisited" tabindex="-1">Demo: Reading progress indicator, revisited</h4>

<p>To recreate the reading progress indicator with JavaScript, while using the same markup, use the following JavaScript code:</p>
<div></div><devsite-code data-copy-event-label=""><pre class="" translate="no" dir="ltr" is-upgraded="" syntax="JavaScript"><code translate="no" dir="ltr"><span class="devsite-syntax-kd">const</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-nx">$progressbar</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-o">=</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-nb">document</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">.</span><span class="devsite-syntax-nx">querySelector</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">(</span><span class="devsite-syntax-s1">'#progress'</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">);</span>

<span class="devsite-syntax-nx">$progressbar</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">.</span><span class="devsite-syntax-nx">style</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">.</span><span class="devsite-syntax-nx">transformOrigin</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-o">=</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-s1">'0% 50%'</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">;</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-nx">$progressbar</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">.</span><span class="devsite-syntax-nx">animate</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">(</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-w">  </span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">{</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-w">    </span><span class="devsite-syntax-nx">transform</span><span class="devsite-syntax-o">:</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">[</span><span class="devsite-syntax-s1">'scaleX(0)'</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">,</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-s1">'scaleX(1)'</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">],</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-w">  </span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">},</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-w">  </span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">{</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-w">    </span><span class="devsite-syntax-nx">fill</span><span class="devsite-syntax-o">:</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-s1">'forwards'</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">,</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-w">    </span><span class="devsite-syntax-nx">timeline</span><span class="devsite-syntax-o">:</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-ow">new</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-nx">ScrollTimeline</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">({</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-w">      </span><span class="devsite-syntax-nx">source</span><span class="devsite-syntax-o">:</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-nb">document</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">.</span><span class="devsite-syntax-nx">documentElement</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">,</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-w">    </span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">}),</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-w">  </span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">}</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-p">);</span>
</code></pre></devsite-code>
<p>The visual result is identical in the CSS version: the created <code translate="no" dir="ltr">timeline</code> tracks the root scroller and scale the <code translate="no" dir="ltr">#progress</code> up on the x-axis from 0% to 100% as you scroll the page.</p>

<p>✨ Try it for yourself
<iframe loading="lazy" src="https://scroll-driven-animations.style/demos/progress-bar/waapi/?embed" frameborder="0" sandbox="allow-scripts allow-forms allow-top-navigation" width="500" height="600" style="height: 600px; width: 100%; border: 1px solid #333;"></iframe></p>

<h2 id="getting_practical_with_view_progress_timeline" data-text="Getting practical with View Progress Timeline" tabindex="-1">Getting practical with View Progress Timeline</h2>

<h3 id="creating_an_anonymous_view_progress_timeline_in_css" data-text="Creating an Anonymous View Progress Timeline in CSS" tabindex="-1">Creating an Anonymous View Progress Timeline in CSS</h3>

<p>To create a View Progress Timeline, use the <code translate="no" dir="ltr">view()</code> function. Its accepted arguments are <code translate="no" dir="ltr"><axis></code> and <code translate="no" dir="ltr"><view-timeline-inset></code>.</p>

<ul>
<li>The <code translate="no" dir="ltr"><axis></code> is the same as from the Scroll Progress Timeline and defines which axis to track. The default value is <code translate="no" dir="ltr">block</code>.</li>
<li>With <code translate="no" dir="ltr"><view-timeline-inset></code>, you can specify an offset <em>(positive or negative)</em> to adjust the bounds when an element is considered to be in view or not. The value must be a percentage or <code translate="no" dir="ltr">auto</code>, with <code translate="no" dir="ltr">auto</code> being the default value.</li>
</ul>

<p>For example, to bind an animation to an element intersecting with its scroller on the block axis, use <code translate="no" dir="ltr">view(block)</code>. Similar to <code translate="no" dir="ltr">scroll()</code>, set this as the value for the <code translate="no" dir="ltr">animation-timeline</code> property and don’t forget to set the <code translate="no" dir="ltr">animation-duration</code> to <code translate="no" dir="ltr">auto</code>.</p>

<p>Using the following code, every <code translate="no" dir="ltr">img</code> will fade-in as it crosses the viewport while you scroll.</p>
<div></div><devsite-code data-copy-event-label=""><pre class="" translate="no" dir="ltr" is-upgraded="" syntax="CSS"><code translate="no" dir="ltr"><span class="devsite-syntax-p">@</span><span class="devsite-syntax-k">keyframes</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-nt">reveal</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">{</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-w">  </span><span class="devsite-syntax-nt">from</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">{</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-k">opacity</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">:</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-mi">0</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">;</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">}</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-w">  </span><span class="devsite-syntax-nt">to</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">{</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-k">opacity</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">:</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-mi">1</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">;</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">}</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-p">}</span>

<span class="devsite-syntax-nt">img</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">{</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-w">  </span><span class="devsite-syntax-k">animation</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">:</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-n">reveal</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-kc">linear</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">;</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-w">  </span><span class="devsite-syntax-n">animation-timeline</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">:</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-nf">view</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">();</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-p">}</span>
</code></pre></devsite-code>
<aside class="key-point"><b>Key point:</b> It is not possible to determine the <code translate="no" dir="ltr"><scroller></code> of a View Timeline, as it always tracks the subject within its nearest parent scroller.</aside>

<h3 id="intermezzo_view_timeline_ranges" data-text="Intermezzo: View Timeline ranges" tabindex="-1">Intermezzo: View Timeline ranges</h3>

<p>By default, an animation linked to the View Timeline attaches to the entire timeline range. This starts from the moment the subject is about to enter the scrollport and ends when the subject has left the scrollport entirely.</p>

<p>It is also possible to link it to a specific part of the View Timeline by specifying the range that it should attach to. This can be, for example, only when the subject is entering the scroller. In the following visualization, the progress starts counting up from 0% when the subject enters the scroll container but already reaches 100% from the moment it is entirely intersecting.</p>

<figure>
  <video controls="" height="800" playsinline="" width="800" style="--vid-width: 800; --vid-height: 800"> <source src="/static/docs/css-ui/scroll-driven-animations/video/AeNB0cHNDkYPUYzDuv8gInYA9rY2/p952qt9FVzv2v3zP0o9Q.mp4" type="video/mp4"> </video>
  <figcaption class="dcc-caption">A View Timeline set to track the entry range of the subject. The animation only runs while the subject is entering the scrollport.</figcaption>
</figure>

<p>The possible View Timeline ranges that you can target are the following:</p>

<ul>
<li><code translate="no" dir="ltr">cover</code>: Represents the full range of the view progress timeline.</li>
<li><code translate="no" dir="ltr">entry</code>: Represents the range during which the principal box is entering the view progress visibility range.</li>
<li><code translate="no" dir="ltr">exit</code>: Represents the range during which the principal box is exiting the view progress visibility range.</li>
<li><code translate="no" dir="ltr">entry-crossing</code>: Represents the range during which the principal box crosses the end border edge.</li>
<li><code translate="no" dir="ltr">exit-crossing</code>: Represents the range during which the principal box crosses the start border edge.</li>
<li><code translate="no" dir="ltr">contain</code>: Represents the range during which the principal box is either fully contained by, or fully covers, its view progress visibility range within the scrollport. This depends on whether the subject is taller or shorter than the scroller.</li>
</ul>

<p>To define a range, you must set a range-start and range-end. Each consists of range-name <em>(see list above)</em> and a range-offset to determine the position within that range-name. The range-offset is typically a percentage ranging from <code translate="no" dir="ltr">0%</code> to <code translate="no" dir="ltr">100%</code> but you can also specify a fixed length such as <code translate="no" dir="ltr">20em</code>.</p>

<p>For example, if you want to run an animation from the moment a subject enters, choose <code translate="no" dir="ltr">entry 0%</code> as the range-start. To have it finished by the time the subject has entered, choose <code translate="no" dir="ltr">entry 100%</code> as a value for the range-end.</p>

<p>In CSS, you set this using the <code translate="no" dir="ltr">animation-range</code> property. Example:</p>
<div></div><devsite-code data-copy-event-label=""><pre class="" translate="no" dir="ltr" is-upgraded="" syntax="CSS"><code translate="no" dir="ltr"><span class="devsite-syntax-nt">animation-range</span><span class="devsite-syntax-o">:</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-nt">entry</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-nt">0</span><span class="devsite-syntax-o">%</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-nt">entry</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-nt">100</span><span class="devsite-syntax-o">%;</span>
</code></pre></devsite-code>
<p>In JavaScript, use the <code translate="no" dir="ltr">rangeStart</code> and <code translate="no" dir="ltr">rangeEnd</code> properties.</p>
<div></div><devsite-code data-copy-event-label=""><pre class="" translate="no" dir="ltr" is-upgraded="" syntax="JavaScript"><code translate="no" dir="ltr"><span class="devsite-syntax-nx">$el</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">.</span><span class="devsite-syntax-nx">animate</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">(</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-w">  </span><span class="devsite-syntax-nx">keyframes</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">,</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-w">  </span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">{</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-w">    </span><span class="devsite-syntax-nx">timeline</span><span class="devsite-syntax-o">:</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-nx">tl</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">,</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-w">    </span><span class="devsite-syntax-nx">rangeStart</span><span class="devsite-syntax-o">:</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-s1">'entry 0%'</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">,</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-w">    </span><span class="devsite-syntax-nx">rangeEnd</span><span class="devsite-syntax-o">:</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-s1">'entry 100%'</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">,</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-w">  </span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">}</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-p">);</span>
</code></pre></devsite-code>
<p>Use the tool embedded below to see what each range-name represents and how the percentages affect the start and end positions. Try to set the range-start to <code translate="no" dir="ltr">entry 0%</code> and the range-end to <code translate="no" dir="ltr">cover 50%</code>, and then drag the scrollbar to see the animation result.</p>

<figure>
  <iframe loading="lazy" src="https://scroll-driven-animations.style/tools/view-timeline/ranges/?embed" frameborder="0" sandbox="allow-scripts allow-forms allow-top-navigation" width="500" height="600" style="height: 600px; width: 100%; border: 1px solid #333;"></iframe>
  <figcaption class="dcc-caption">The View Timeline Ranges Visualizer, available at <a href="https://goo.gle/view-timeline-range-tool">https://goo.gle/view-timeline-range-tool</a></figcaption>
</figure>

<p>Watch a recording
<video controls="" height="800" playsinline="" width="800" style="--vid-width: 800; --vid-height: 800"> <source src="/static/docs/css-ui/scroll-driven-animations/video/AeNB0cHNDkYPUYzDuv8gInYA9rY2/x6SLvlnns26zNGeJOCuS.mp4" type="video/mp4"> </video></p>

<p>As you might notice while playing around with this View Timeline Ranges tools, some ranges can be targeted by two different range-name + range-offset combinations. For example, <code translate="no" dir="ltr">entry 0%</code>, <code translate="no" dir="ltr">entry-crossing 0%</code>, and <code translate="no" dir="ltr">cover 0%</code> all target the same area.</p>

<p>When the range-start and range-end target the same range-name and span the entire range–from 0% up to 100%–you can shorten the value to simply the range name. For example, <code translate="no" dir="ltr">animation-range: entry 0% entry 100%;</code> can be rewritten to the much shorter <code translate="no" dir="ltr">animation-range: entry</code>.</p>

<aside class="key-point"><b>Key point:</b> Note that these ranges are derived from the untransformed <em>principal box</em> of the subject. That means that transformations such as <code translate="no" dir="ltr">scale</code> and <code translate="no" dir="ltr">translate</code> are not taken into account when deriving the ranges. This is a good thing, as this allows you to scale a subject during scroll without affecting the available scroll estate. If the transformed box were used, attached animations would flicker because they would constantly need to be recalculated in response to a change in scroll estate.</aside>

<h4 id="demo_image_reveal" data-text="Demo: Image reveal" tabindex="-1">Demo: Image reveal</h4>

<p>This demo fades in the images as they enter the scrollport. This is done using an Anonymous View Timeline. The animation range has been tweaked so that each image is at full opacity when it is halfway the scroller.</p>

<figure>
  <video controls="" height="800" playsinline="" width="800" style="--vid-width: 800; --vid-height: 800"> <source src="/static/docs/css-ui/scroll-driven-animations/video/AeNB0cHNDkYPUYzDuv8gInYA9rY2/eV0R7BXHO7ieTFVBfTEE.mp4" type="video/mp4"> </video>
  <figcaption class="dcc-caption">Demo: <a href="https://scroll-driven-animations.style/demos/image-reveal/css/anonymous.html">Image reveal</a></figcaption>
</figure>

<p>✨ Try it for yourself
<iframe loading="lazy" src="https://scroll-driven-animations.style/demos/image-reveal/css/anonymous.html?embed" frameborder="0" sandbox="allow-scripts allow-forms allow-top-navigation" width="500" height="600" style="height: 600px; width: 100%; border: 1px solid #333;"></iframe></p>

<p>The expanding effect is achieved by using a clip-path that is animated. The CSS used for this effect is this:</p>
<div></div><devsite-code data-copy-event-label=""><pre class="" translate="no" dir="ltr" is-upgraded="" syntax="CSS"><code translate="no" dir="ltr"><span class="devsite-syntax-p">@</span><span class="devsite-syntax-k">keyframes</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-nt">reveal</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">{</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-w">  </span><span class="devsite-syntax-nt">from</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">{</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-k">opacity</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">:</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-mi">0</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">;</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-k">clip-path</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">:</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-nb">inset</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">(</span><span class="devsite-syntax-mi">0</span><span class="devsite-syntax-kt">%</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-mi">60</span><span class="devsite-syntax-kt">%</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-mi">0</span><span class="devsite-syntax-kt">%</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-mi">50</span><span class="devsite-syntax-kt">%</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">);</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">}</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-w">  </span><span class="devsite-syntax-nt">to</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">{</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-k">opacity</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">:</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-mi">1</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">;</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-k">clip-path</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">:</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-nb">inset</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">(</span><span class="devsite-syntax-mi">0</span><span class="devsite-syntax-kt">%</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-mi">0</span><span class="devsite-syntax-kt">%</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-mi">0</span><span class="devsite-syntax-kt">%</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-mi">0</span><span class="devsite-syntax-kt">%</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">);</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">}</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-p">}</span>

<span class="devsite-syntax-p">.</span><span class="devsite-syntax-nc">revealing-image</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">{</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-w">  </span><span class="devsite-syntax-k">animation</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">:</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-kc">auto</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-kc">linear</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-n">reveal</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-kc">both</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">;</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-w">  </span><span class="devsite-syntax-n">animation-timeline</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">:</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-nf">view</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">();</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-w">  </span><span class="devsite-syntax-n">animation-range</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">:</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-n">entry</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-mi">25</span><span class="devsite-syntax-kt">%</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-kc">cover</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-mi">50</span><span class="devsite-syntax-kt">%</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">;</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-p">}</span>
</code></pre></devsite-code>
<h3 id="creating_a_named_view_progress_timeline_in_css" data-text="Creating a named View Progress Timeline in CSS" tabindex="-1">Creating a named View Progress Timeline in CSS</h3>

<p>Similar to how Scroll Timelines have named versions, you can also create named View Timelines. Instead of the <code translate="no" dir="ltr">scroll-timeline-*</code> properties you use variants that carry the <code translate="no" dir="ltr">view-timeline-</code> prefix, namely <code translate="no" dir="ltr">view-timeline-name</code> and <code translate="no" dir="ltr">view-timeline-axis</code>.</p>

<p>The same type of values apply, and the same rules for looking up a named timeline apply.</p>

<h4 id="demo_image_reveal_revisited" data-text="Demo: Image reveal, revisited" tabindex="-1">Demo: Image reveal, revisited</h4>

<p>Reworking the image reveal demo from earlier, the revised code looks like this:</p>
<div></div><devsite-code data-copy-event-label=""><pre class="" translate="no" dir="ltr" is-upgraded="" syntax="CSS"><code translate="no" dir="ltr"><span class="devsite-syntax-p">.</span><span class="devsite-syntax-nc">revealing-image</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">{</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-w">  </span><span class="devsite-syntax-n">view-timeline-name</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">:</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-o">--</span><span class="devsite-syntax-n">revealing-image</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">;</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-w">  </span><span class="devsite-syntax-n">view-timeline-axis</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">:</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-kc">block</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">;</span>

<span class="devsite-syntax-w">  </span><span class="devsite-syntax-k">animation</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">:</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-kc">auto</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-kc">linear</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-n">reveal</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-kc">both</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">;</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-w">  </span><span class="devsite-syntax-n">animation-timeline</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">:</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-o">--</span><span class="devsite-syntax-n">revealing-image</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">;</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-w">  </span><span class="devsite-syntax-n">animation-range</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">:</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-n">entry</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-mi">25</span><span class="devsite-syntax-kt">%</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-kc">cover</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-mi">50</span><span class="devsite-syntax-kt">%</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">;</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-p">}</span>
</code></pre></devsite-code>
<p>Using <code translate="no" dir="ltr">view-timeline-name: revealing-image</code>, the element will be tracked within its nearest scroller. The same value is then used as the value for the <code translate="no" dir="ltr">animation-timeline</code> property. The visual output is exactly the same as before.</p>

<p>✨ Try it for yourself
<iframe loading="lazy" src="https://scroll-driven-animations.style/demos/image-reveal/css/?embed" frameborder="0" sandbox="allow-scripts allow-forms allow-top-navigation" width="500" height="600" style="height: 600px; width: 100%; border: 1px solid #333;"></iframe></p>

<h3 id="creating_a_view_progress_timeline_in_javascript" data-text="Creating a View Progress Timeline in JavaScript" tabindex="-1">Creating a View Progress Timeline in JavaScript</h3>

<p>To create a View Timeline in JavaScript, create a new instance of the <code translate="no" dir="ltr">ViewTimeline</code> class. Pass in a property bag with the <code translate="no" dir="ltr">subject</code> that you want to track, <code translate="no" dir="ltr">axis</code>, and <code translate="no" dir="ltr">inset</code>.</p>

<ul>
<li><code translate="no" dir="ltr">subject</code>: A reference to the element that you want to track within its own scroller.</li>
<li><code translate="no" dir="ltr">axis</code>: The axis to track. Similar to the CSS variant, accepted values are <code translate="no" dir="ltr">block</code>, <code translate="no" dir="ltr">inline</code>, <code translate="no" dir="ltr">x</code>, and <code translate="no" dir="ltr">y</code>.</li>
<li><code translate="no" dir="ltr">inset</code>: An inset <em>(positive)</em> or outset <em>(negative)</em> adjustment of the scrollport when determining whether the box is in view.</li>
</ul>
<div></div><devsite-code data-copy-event-label=""><pre class="" translate="no" dir="ltr" is-upgraded="" syntax="JavaScript"><code translate="no" dir="ltr"><span class="devsite-syntax-kd">const</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-nx">tl</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-o">=</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-ow">new</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-nx">ViewTimeline</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">({</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-w">  </span><span class="devsite-syntax-nx">subject</span><span class="devsite-syntax-o">:</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-nb">document</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">.</span><span class="devsite-syntax-nx">getElementById</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">(</span><span class="devsite-syntax-s1">'subject'</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">),</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-p">});</span>
</code></pre></devsite-code>
<p>To attach it to a Web Animation, pass it in as the <code translate="no" dir="ltr">timeline</code> property and omit any <code translate="no" dir="ltr">duration</code> if there was any. Optionally, pass in range information using the <code translate="no" dir="ltr">rangeStart</code> and <code translate="no" dir="ltr">rangeEnd</code> properties.</p>
<div></div><devsite-code data-copy-event-label=""><pre class="" translate="no" dir="ltr" is-upgraded="" syntax="JavaScript"><code translate="no" dir="ltr"><span class="devsite-syntax-nx">$el</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">.</span><span class="devsite-syntax-nx">animate</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">({</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-w">  </span><span class="devsite-syntax-nx">opacity</span><span class="devsite-syntax-o">:</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">[</span><span class="devsite-syntax-mf">0</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">,</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-mf">1</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">],</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-p">},</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">{</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-w">  </span><span class="devsite-syntax-nx">timeline</span><span class="devsite-syntax-o">:</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-nx">tl</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">,</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-w">  </span><span class="devsite-syntax-nx">rangeStart</span><span class="devsite-syntax-o">:</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-s1">'entry 25%'</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">,</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-w">  </span><span class="devsite-syntax-nx">rangeEnd</span><span class="devsite-syntax-o">:</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-s1">'cover 50%'</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">,</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-p">});</span>
</code></pre></devsite-code>
<p>✨ Try it for yourself
<iframe loading="lazy" src="https://scroll-driven-animations.style/demos/image-reveal/waapi/?embed" frameborder="0" sandbox="allow-scripts allow-forms allow-top-navigation" width="500" height="600" style="height: 600px; width: 100%; border: 1px solid #333;"></iframe></p>

<aside class="note"><b>Note:</b> The animated element <code translate="no" dir="ltr">$el</code> and the <code translate="no" dir="ltr">subject</code> do not need to be the same element. This means that you can track an element in its scroller while animating a distant element somewhere else in the DOM tree.</aside>

<h2 id="more_things_to_try_out" data-text="More things to try out" tabindex="-1">More things to try out</h2>

<h3 id="attaching_to_multiple_view_timeline_ranges_with_one_set_of_keyframes" data-text="Attaching to multiple View Timeline ranges with one set of keyframes" tabindex="-1">Attaching to multiple View Timeline ranges with one set of keyframes</h3>

<p>Let’s take a look at this contact list demo where the list entries are animated. As a list entry enters the scrollport from the bottom it slides+fades in, and as it exits the scrollport at the top it slides+fades out.</p>

<figure>
  <video controls="" height="800" playsinline="" width="800" style="--vid-width: 800; --vid-height: 800"> <source src="/static/docs/css-ui/scroll-driven-animations/video/AeNB0cHNDkYPUYzDuv8gInYA9rY2/edztzaUNSuQsUVUzF9vr.mp4" type="video/mp4"> </video>
  <figcaption class="dcc-caption">Demo: <a href="https://scroll-driven-animations.style/demos/contact-list/css/multiple-animations.html">Contact list</a></figcaption>
</figure>

<p>✨ Try it for yourself
<iframe loading="lazy" src="https://scroll-driven-animations.style/demos/contact-list/css/multiple-animations.html?embed" frameborder="0" sandbox="allow-scripts allow-forms allow-top-navigation" width="500" height="600" style="height: 600px; width: 100%; border: 1px solid #333;"></iframe></p>

<p>For this demo, each element gets decorated with one View Timeline that tracks the element as it crosses its scrollport yet two scroll-driven animations are attached to it. The <code translate="no" dir="ltr">animate-in</code> animation is attached to the <code translate="no" dir="ltr">entry</code> range of the timeline, and the <code translate="no" dir="ltr">animate-out</code> animation to the <code translate="no" dir="ltr">exit</code> range of the timeline.</p>
<div></div><devsite-code data-copy-event-label=""><pre class="" translate="no" dir="ltr" is-upgraded="" syntax="CSS"><code translate="no" dir="ltr"><span class="devsite-syntax-p">@</span><span class="devsite-syntax-k">keyframes</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-nt">animate-in</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">{</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-w">  </span><span class="devsite-syntax-nt">0</span><span class="devsite-syntax-o">%</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">{</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-k">opacity</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">:</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-mi">0</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">;</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-k">transform</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">:</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-nb">translateY</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">(</span><span class="devsite-syntax-mi">100</span><span class="devsite-syntax-kt">%</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">);</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">}</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-w">  </span><span class="devsite-syntax-nt">100</span><span class="devsite-syntax-o">%</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">{</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-k">opacity</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">:</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-mi">1</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">;</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-k">transform</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">:</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-nb">translateY</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">(</span><span class="devsite-syntax-mi">0</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">);</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">}</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-p">}</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-p">@</span><span class="devsite-syntax-k">keyframes</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-nt">animate-out</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">{</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-w">  </span><span class="devsite-syntax-nt">0</span><span class="devsite-syntax-o">%</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">{</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-k">opacity</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">:</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-mi">1</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">;</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-k">transform</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">:</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-nb">translateY</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">(</span><span class="devsite-syntax-mi">0</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">);</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">}</span>
