<span class="devsite-syntax-w">  </span><span class="devsite-syntax-nt">100</span><span class="devsite-syntax-o">%</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">{</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-k">opacity</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">:</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-mi">0</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">;</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-k">transform</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">:</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-nb">translateY</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">(</span><span class="devsite-syntax-mi">-100</span><span class="devsite-syntax-kt">%</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">);</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">}</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-p">}</span>

<span class="devsite-syntax-p">#</span><span class="devsite-syntax-nn">list-view</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-nt">li</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">{</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-w">  </span><span class="devsite-syntax-k">animation</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">:</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-n">animate-in</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-kc">linear</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-kc">forwards</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">,</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-w">             </span><span class="devsite-syntax-n">animate-out</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-kc">linear</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-kc">forwards</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">;</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-w">  </span><span class="devsite-syntax-n">animation-timeline</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">:</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-nf">view</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">();</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-w">  </span><span class="devsite-syntax-n">animation-range</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">:</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-n">entry</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">,</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-n">exit</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">;</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-p">}</span>
</code></pre></devsite-code>
<p>Instead of running two different animations attached to two different ranges, it is also possible to create one set of keyframes that already contains the range information.</p>
<div></div><devsite-code data-copy-event-label=""><pre class="" translate="no" dir="ltr" is-upgraded="" syntax="CSS"><code translate="no" dir="ltr"><span class="devsite-syntax-p">@</span><span class="devsite-syntax-k">keyframes</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-nt">animate-in-and-out</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">{</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-w">  </span><span class="devsite-syntax-nt">entry</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-nt">0</span><span class="devsite-syntax-o">%</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w">  </span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">{</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-w">    </span><span class="devsite-syntax-k">opacity</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">:</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-mi">0</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">;</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-k">transform</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">:</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-nb">translateY</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">(</span><span class="devsite-syntax-mi">100</span><span class="devsite-syntax-kt">%</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">);</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-w">  </span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">}</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-w">  </span><span class="devsite-syntax-nt">entry</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-nt">100</span><span class="devsite-syntax-o">%</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w">  </span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">{</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-w">    </span><span class="devsite-syntax-k">opacity</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">:</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-mi">1</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">;</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-k">transform</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">:</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-nb">translateY</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">(</span><span class="devsite-syntax-mi">0</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">);</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-w">  </span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">}</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-w">  </span><span class="devsite-syntax-nt">exit</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-nt">0</span><span class="devsite-syntax-o">%</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">{</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-w">    </span><span class="devsite-syntax-k">opacity</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">:</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-mi">1</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">;</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-k">transform</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">:</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-nb">translateY</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">(</span><span class="devsite-syntax-mi">0</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">);</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-w">  </span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">}</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-w">  </span><span class="devsite-syntax-nt">exit</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-nt">100</span><span class="devsite-syntax-o">%</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">{</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-w">    </span><span class="devsite-syntax-k">opacity</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">:</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-mi">0</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">;</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-k">transform</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">:</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-nb">translateY</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">(</span><span class="devsite-syntax-mi">-100</span><span class="devsite-syntax-kt">%</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">);</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-w">  </span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">}</span>

<span class="devsite-syntax-p">#</span><span class="devsite-syntax-nn">list-view</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-nt">li</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">{</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-w">  </span><span class="devsite-syntax-k">animation</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">:</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-kc">linear</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-n">animate-in-and-out</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">;</span>

<iframe loading="lazy" src="https://scroll-driven-animations.style/demos/contact-list/css/?embed" frameborder="0" sandbox="allow-scripts allow-forms allow-top-navigation" width="500" height="600" style="height: 600px; width: 100%; border: 1px solid #333;"></iframe></p>

<h3 id="attaching_to_a_non-ancestor_scroll_timeline" data-text="Attaching to a non-ancestor Scroll Timeline" tabindex="-1">Attaching to a non-ancestor Scroll Timeline</h3>

<aside class="note"><b>Note:</b> The feature described in this section is not supported in Chrome 115. To try it out, use Chrome 116 with the Experimental Web Platform Features flag enabled.</aside>

<p>The lookup mechanism for named Scroll Timelines and named View Timelines is limited to scroll ancestors only. Very often though, the element that needs to be animated is not a child of the scroller that needs to be tracked.</p>

<p>To make this work, the <code translate="no" dir="ltr">timeline-scope</code> property comes into play. You use this property to declare a timeline with that name without actually creating it. This gives the timeline with that name a broader scope. In practice, you use the <code translate="no" dir="ltr">timeline-scope</code> property on a shared parent element so that a child scroller’s timeline can attach to it.</p>

<p>For example:</p>
<div></div><devsite-code data-copy-event-label=""><pre class="" translate="no" dir="ltr" is-upgraded="" syntax="CSS"><code translate="no" dir="ltr"><span class="devsite-syntax-p">.</span><span class="devsite-syntax-nc">parent</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">{</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-w">  </span><span class="devsite-syntax-n">timeline-scope</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">:</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-o">--</span><span class="devsite-syntax-n">tl</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">;</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-p">}</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-p">.</span><span class="devsite-syntax-nc">parent</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">.</span><span class="devsite-syntax-nc">scroller</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">{</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-w">  </span><span class="devsite-syntax-n">scroll-timeline</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">:</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-o">--</span><span class="devsite-syntax-n">tl</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">;</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-p">}</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-p">.</span><span class="devsite-syntax-nc">parent</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">.</span><span class="devsite-syntax-nc">scroller</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-o">~</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">.</span><span class="devsite-syntax-nc">subject</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">{</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-w">  </span><span class="devsite-syntax-k">animation</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">:</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-n">animate</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-kc">linear</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">;</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-w">  </span><span class="devsite-syntax-n">animation-timeline</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">:</span><span class="devsite-syntax-w"> </span><span class="devsite-syntax-o">--</span><span class="devsite-syntax-n">tl</span><span class="devsite-syntax-p">;</span>
<span class="devsite-syntax-p">}</span>
</code></pre></devsite-code>
<p>In this snippet:</p>

<ul>
<li>The <code translate="no" dir="ltr">.parent</code> element declares a timeline with the name <code translate="no" dir="ltr">--tl</code>. Any child of it can find and use it as a value for the <code translate="no" dir="ltr">animation-timeline</code> property.</li>
<li>The <code translate="no" dir="ltr">.scroller</code> element actually defines a Scroll Timeline with the name <code translate="no" dir="ltr">--tl</code>. By default it would only be visible to its children but because <code translate="no" dir="ltr">.parent</code> has it set as the <code translate="no" dir="ltr">scroll-timeline-root</code>, it attaches to it.</li>
<li>The <code translate="no" dir="ltr">.subject</code> element uses the <code translate="no" dir="ltr">--tl</code> timeline. It walks up its ancestor tree and finds <code translate="no" dir="ltr">--tl</code> on the <code translate="no" dir="ltr">.parent</code>. With the <code translate="no" dir="ltr">--tl</code> on the <code translate="no" dir="ltr">.parent</code> pointing to the <code translate="no" dir="ltr">--tl</code> of <code translate="no" dir="ltr">.scroller</code>, the <code translate="no" dir="ltr">.subject</code> will essentially track the <code translate="no" dir="ltr">.scroller</code>’s Scroll Progress Timeline.</li>
</ul>

<p>Put differently, you can use <code translate="no" dir="ltr">timeline-root</code> to move a timeline up to an ancestor (aka <em>hoisting</em>), so that all children of the ancestor can access it.</p>

<p>The <code translate="no" dir="ltr">timeline-scope</code> property can be used with both both Scroll Timelines and View Timelines.</p>

<h2 id="more_demos_and_resources" data-text="More demos and resources" tabindex="-1">More demos and resources</h2>

<p>All demos covered in this article on <a href="https://scroll-driven-animations.style/">the scroll-driven-animations.style mini-site</a>. The website includes many more demos to highlight what is possible with Scroll-driven animations.</p>

<p>One of the additional demos is this list of album covers. Each cover rotates in 3D as it takes the center spotlight.</p>

<figure>
  <video controls="" height="800" playsinline="" width="800" style="--vid-width: 800; --vid-height: 800"> <source src="/static/docs/css-ui/scroll-driven-animations/video/AeNB0cHNDkYPUYzDuv8gInYA9rY2/hiItR1vueBVTJbgOAwHp.mp4" type="video/mp4"> </video>
  <figcaption class="dcc-caption">Demo: <a href="https://scroll-driven-animations.style/demos/cover-flow/css/">Cover Flow</a></figcaption>
</figure>

<p>✨ Try it for yourself
<iframe loading="lazy" src="https://scroll-driven-animations.style/demos/cover-flow/css/?embed" frameborder="0" sandbox="allow-scripts allow-forms allow-top-navigation" width="500" height="600" style="height: 600px; width: 100%; border: 1px solid #333;"></iframe></p>

<p>Or this stacking cards demo that leverage <code translate="no" dir="ltr">position: sticky</code>. As the cards stack, the already stuck cards scale down, creating a nice depth effect. In the end, the entire stack slides out of view as a group.</p>

<figure>
  <video controls="" height="800" playsinline="" width="800" style="--vid-width: 800; --vid-height: 800"> <source src="/static/docs/css-ui/scroll-driven-animations/video/AeNB0cHNDkYPUYzDuv8gInYA9rY2/dyy4rOs0WsWOAuxS9sBG.mp4" type="video/mp4"> </video>
  <figcaption class="dcc-caption">Demo: <a href="https://scroll-driven-animations.style/demos/stacking-cards/css/">Stacking cards</a><a>.</a></figcaption><a>
</a></figure><a>

<p>✨ Try it for yourself
<iframe loading="lazy" src="https://scroll-driven-animations.style/demos/stacking-cards/css/?embed" frameborder="0" sandbox="allow-scripts allow-forms allow-top-navigation" width="500" height="600" style="height: 600px; width: 100%; border: 1px solid #333;"></iframe></p>

</a><p><a>Also featured on </a><a href="https://scroll-driven-animations.style/">scroll-driven-animations.style</a> is a collection of tools such as the View Timeline Range Progress visualization that was included earlier in this post.</p>

<p>Scroll-driven animations are also covered in <a href="https://io.google/2023/program/170b44dc-47f0-409f-92fe-b5c70aab4569/">What’s new in Web Animations</a> at Google I/O ’23.</p>
